topic: Solo bookkeeper cuts $8k/month in subs with $97 workflow
hook_summary: I replaced $8,000 a month in subcontractors with a $97 automation.

scenes.audio_text: I replaced $8,000 a month in subcontractors with a $97 automation. My clients didn't even notice.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: medium close-up, 35mm, eye-level, seated leaning forward with both forearms resting flat on desk, direct deadpan eye contact held for 1.5 beats after '$8,000' [2] SETTING: warm wood writing desk in a quiet home study, tall bookshelf packed with binders and paperbacks behind him, a ceramic mug and a small open Moleskine visible on the desk surface — @daniel in light denim shirt under a tan suede jacket [3] LIGHT: single warm incandescent desk lamp to his left casting a soft amber pool, late evening, deep shadows on the right side of frame [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: pages of a book flutter faintly from a nearby vent, a wall clock's second hand ticks visibly over his shoulder, ambient lamp flicker from the fireplace in the adjacent room spills through the doorway [5] CAMERA + MOOD: slow 1-foot push-in on a handheld rig, barely perceptible drift left, intimate confessional energy — the stillness of someone about to say something they know will land hard

scenes.audio_text: I had four bookkeeping subs. Good people. But by March I was clearing less than they were.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: medium shot, 50mm, slight low angle looking up, seated on the edge of the bed with one elbow on knee and chin resting on closed fist [2] SETTING: bedroom edge of bed, morning light pouring through sheer linen curtains casting long soft stripes across the hardwood floor, a phone face-down and an open laptop on the bed behind him — @daniel in light denim shirt under a tan suede jacket [3] LIGHT: diffuse early-morning window light from the east, cool-warm mix, no artificial fill, soft shadow under jawline [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: curtain fabric shifts gently in the draft from a cracked window, dust motes drifting through the light beam, a ceiling fan rotates slowly above [5] CAMERA + MOOD: slow handheld drift rightward, subject stays centered, quiet morning-reckoning energy — like a decision already made the night before

scenes.audio_text: Every deliverable ran through three inboxes. Five days from close to client report. Every single month.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: wide shot, 28mm, eye-level, standing with arms loosely crossed, weight shifted to one hip, slight tilt of head [2] SETTING: garage workshop with a pegboard of hanging tools behind him, a single aluminum work lamp clamped to the edge of a cluttered workbench, oil-stained concrete floor — @daniel in light denim shirt under a tan suede jacket [3] LIGHT: single hard practical work lamp from above-right at roughly 45 degrees, casting strong directional shadows, rest of the garage in dim ambient [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: a shop fan oscillates slowly in the far corner moving dust across the floor, an overhead fluorescent tube flickers briefly, a rag draped over the bench edge sways from the fan's draft [5] CAMERA + MOOD: static wide locked with a slow 2-foot tracking pull backward, revealing the space around him, tone of frustrated retrospect — someone describing a system they should have fixed sooner

scenes.audio_text: I built one Make.com workflow connected to Claude. Expected it to break on the first real client file. It didn't.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: over-shoulder wide, 35mm, slight high angle looking down at the kitchen counter, both hands flat on the island surface leaning forward [2] SETTING: kitchen island at dusk, one brushed-brass pendant lamp switched on above the island, a laptop open at the counter, a half-drunk glass of water, takeout container pushed to the side — @daniel in light denim shirt under a tan suede jacket [3] LIGHT: warm single pendant lamp overhead, deep blue dusk visible through the window above the sink, cool ambient from outside contrasting the warm cone of light [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: a kettle on the back burner vents a thin thread of steam, exterior trees sway in wind visible through the kitchen window, a dripping faucet sends ripples across the sink basin [5] CAMERA + MOOD: slow dolly in from wide to medium, camera settling just as he says 'It didn't' — quiet disbelief turned into conviction, the energy of someone who ran the test and was genuinely surprised

scenes.audio_text: Turnaround dropped to four hours. Same quarter, I raised my prices 40%. Nobody asked why.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: medium close-up, 85mm portrait, eye-level, leaning back into leather armchair with one arm resting along the side, relaxed open posture [2] SETTING: leather armchair beside a fireplace, low orange firelight from burning logs, a short glass of water on the side table, framed print on the wall partially lit — @daniel in light denim shirt under a tan suede jacket [3] LIGHT: warm firelight as primary practical source, dancing and shifting naturally, no fill light, deep warm shadows across the left side of his face [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: fire logs actively burning with visible flame movement and ember glow, shadows from the flame shift across the wall and ceiling, a candle on the mantel flickers in the convection current [5] CAMERA + MOOD: slow handheld orbit left-to-right about 20 degrees, subject stays in frame, fire dancing behind him — measured, almost amused delivery, the energy of someone who expected pushback and got silence instead

scenes.audio_text: The work didn't get worse. The overhead just disappeared. That's the only math that matters.
scenes.visual_prompt: [1] COMPOSITION: medium shot, 50mm, eye-level, seated in reading nook with one hand on chin, half-turned toward camera from a slight three-quarter angle [2] SETTING: reading nook with a cushioned window bench, rain visible streaking the glass behind him, a closed hardback book on his knee, a dim floor lamp to the right — @daniel in light denim shirt under a tan suede jacket [3] LIGHT: overcast diffuse daylight from the rain-streaked window, grey-cool and flat from outside, balanced by a warm floor lamp on the right creating a split-tone effect [4] LIVE BACKGROUND: rain droplets stream visibly down the window glass behind him, a tree branch outside sways in the wind, a candle on the windowsill throws a faint waver of light onto the sill [5] CAMERA + MOOD: very slow 1-foot push-in, handheld with minimal drift, subject quietly holds eye contact through the final line — the tone of someone who figured something out and is simply stating it, no performance, just fact

loop_back_line: The work didn't get worse. The overhead just disappeared. That's the only math that matters.
